SRC-0001.title: Delaware Division of Corporations Annual Report and Franchise Tax
SRC-0001.authority: State of Delaware Division of Corporations

SRC-0002.title: Florida Division of Corporations File Annual Report
SRC-0002.authority: Florida Department of State Division of Corporations

SRC-0004.title: New York Department of State Biennial Statements

governing_law.basis: Delaware entity maintenance and franchise-tax routing are anchored to the Division of Corporations source in SRC-0001.

forum.basis: Delaware entity status, registered-agent, and filing authority questions route to the Division of Corporations channel in SRC-0001.

tax.basis: Domestic corporations file annual reports and franchise tax through SRC-0001; Delaware LLC, LP, and GP annual tax routing is also stated in SRC-0001.

licenses.basis: Entity maintenance is source-backed by SRC-0001; separate operating licenses must be routed by activity after intake identifies the regulated business line.

filings.basis: Annual report, amended annual report, foreign corporation annual report, and LLC or partnership tax obligations are mapped to SRC-0001.

deadlines.desc: Domestic corporation annual report and franchise-tax receipt deadline.
Delaware LLC, LP, and GP annual tax deadline.

jurisdictions.name: Florida
jurisdictions.role: foreign or domestic registration and annual-report authority

governing_law.basis: Florida annual-report and active-status obligations for corporations, LLCs, LPs, and LLLPs are mapped to SRC-0002.

forum.basis: Florida Division of Corporations records, entity status, filing confirmation, and dissolution or revocation paths route through SRC-0002.

tax.basis: SRC-0002 states annual-report payment handling and late-fee consequences; tax return work must route separately to the owning tax seat.

licenses.basis: SRC-0002 identifies annual-report maintenance and points donation-soliciting nonprofits to Florida FDACS registration where applicable.

filings.basis: Annual report, amended annual report, reinstatement, dissolution, withdrawal, and status-certificate workflows are source-backed by SRC-0002.

deadlines.desc: For profit corporations, LLCs, LPs, and LLLPs, file by May 1 to avoid the annual-report late fee described by the Florida Division of Corporations.
Annual reports are due by the third Friday in September to avoid administrative dissolution or revocation in Florida records.

jurisdictions.name: Texas
jurisdictions.role: franchise-tax authority and entity account-status regulator

governing_law.basis: Texas franchise-tax applicability for taxable entities formed, organized, or doing business in Texas is mapped to SRC-0003.

forum.basis: Account-status, tax-certificate, termination, conversion, merger, and withdrawal routing are mapped to Comptroller processes in SRC-0003.

tax.basis: Texas franchise tax report, payment, extension, threshold, and rate references are source-backed by SRC-0003.

licenses.basis: SRC-0003 covers franchise-tax account status; activity-specific licenses must route from intake facts to the responsible Texas regulator.

filings.basis: Annual franchise-tax reports, final franchise-tax reports, extensions, common-owner information, and account updates are mapped to SRC-0003.

deadlines.desc: Annual Texas franchise-tax report due date, moving to the next business day if May 15 falls on a weekend or holiday.
Out-of-state entity final report and payment deadline after ceasing Texas nexus.

governing_law.basis: New York business-corporation and LLC biennial-statement obligations are mapped to Department of State guidance in SRC-0004.

forum.basis: Service-of-process address records, certificate status impact, and Department of State filing route are mapped to SRC-0004.

tax.basis: SRC-0004 is an entity-record source; New York tax filings must route separately to the tax seat after intake identifies tax type and nexus.

licenses.basis: Biennial statement and status-record duties are source-backed by SRC-0004; licenses require activity-specific routing after intake.

filings.basis: Biennial statements, amendments, online filing, fee, and past-due remediation paths are mapped to SRC-0004.

deadlines.due: Calendar month of formation or authorization every two years
deadlines.desc: Business corporations and LLCs file the biennial statement during the calendar month in which the original formation or authority filing occurred.

governing_law.basis: Federal corporate income-tax return routing for corporations is anchored to IRS Form 1120 source material in SRC-0005.

forum.basis: SRC-0005 maps federal filing authority, not contract forum selection; disputes route to tax controversy or litigation seats by matter facts.

tax.basis: Form 1120 corporate income-tax filing, schedules, instructions, and related IRS materials are mapped to SRC-0005.

licenses.basis: SRC-0005 is a federal tax-form authority; federal operating licenses must be routed from activity facts to the responsible regulator.

filings.basis: Corporate income-tax return preparation and related form instruction routing are mapped to SRC-0005.

deadlines.due: Entity-specific tax-year deadline from current IRS Form 1120 instructions
deadlines.desc: Before filing, compute the federal corporate return deadline from the taxpayer year-end, extension posture, and current IRS instructions linked from SRC-0005.
